title: eriogonum diatomaceum
intro: Eriogonum diatomaceum, also known as Mormon tea or alkali sage, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, with a history of being used by Native Americans for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are used to make a herbal tea that has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties and high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The tea has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and acting as an antibacterial agent. Eriogonum diatomaceum can be prepared as a tea by steeping the dried leaves and stems in hot water, or it can be made into a tincture or infusion for easier consumption.
